mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the mechanism Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate labor market resilience and rising aggregate income, supporting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the inflation signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand expansion, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficients become statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity crunch regime signals supply chain bottlenecks and forced deleveraging, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during financial stress periods, or the signal hit rate drops below 40% in regime-filtered backtests.
